questions.testName: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A district school board in a certain state is proposing a change to the time school starts for all high schools in the district. A sample of 353 high school students was selected at random from all high school students in the district. The selected students were asked whether they approved of the proposed change, and 300 students responded that they did not approve. Which of the following is the largest population to which the results of the survey can be generalized?
questions.questionText: A district school board in a certain state is proposing a change to the time school starts for all high schools in the district. A sample of 353 high school students was selected at random from all high school students in the district. The selected students were asked whether they approved of the proposed change, and 300 students responded that they did not approve. Which of the following is the largest population to which the results of the survey can be generalized?

choices.text: All high school students in the district
The 300 students who responded that they did not approve of the proposed change
All high school students in the state

questions.testName: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The Mars Perseverance rover is a robot designed to collect samples of rock and soil. During an experiment, the rover moves at a constant speed on a straight path toward a designated target. The equation $ y = 390 - 142x $ gives the rover's remaining distance from its target $ y $, in meters, $ x $ hours after the start of the experiment. What is the best interpretation of 390 in this context?
questions.questionText: The Mars Perseverance rover is a robot designed to collect samples of rock and soil. During an experiment, the rover moves at a constant speed on a straight path toward a designated target. The equation $ y = 390 - 142x $ gives the rover's remaining distance from its target $ y $, in meters, $ x $ hours after the start of the experiment. What is the best interpretation of 390 in this context?

choices.text: The number of hours it takes the rover to reach its target
The rover's speed, in meters per hour, during the experiment
The rover's distance, in meters, from its target at the start of the experiment
The total distance, in meters, the rover travels in 1 hour

questions.testName: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1

questions.testName: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A company that produces only one type of item wants to estimate the percent of the items produced in a typical week that are defective. A random sample of 400 of the items produced in a certain week were tested. Based on the sample, it is estimated that 8.5% of all items produced by the company in this week are defective, with an associated margin of error of 2.73%. Based on the estimate and associated margin of error, which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion about all items produced by the company during this week?
questions.questionText: A company that produces only one type of item wants to estimate the percent of the items produced in a typical week that are defective. A random sample of 400 of the items produced in a certain week were tested. Based on the sample, it is estimated that 8.5% of all items produced by the company in this week are defective, with an associated margin of error of 2.73%. Based on the estimate and associated margin of error, which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion about all items produced by the company during this week?

choices.text: It is plausible that between 5.77% and 11.23% of the items are defective.
It is plausible that more than 11.23% of the items are defective.

category.subject: Math
category.skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error

questions.testName: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The equation $y = -4.9(x - 9.1)^2 + 10,700$ gives the estimated height above ground, $y$, in meters, of a plane, where $x$ is the number of seconds since it started a parabolic maneuver. If this equation is graphed in the $xy$-plane, which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph?
questions.questionText: The equation $y = -4.9(x - 9.1)^2 + 10,700$ gives the estimated height above ground, $y$, in meters, of a plane, where $x$ is the number of seconds since it started a parabolic maneuver. If this equation is graphed in the $xy$-plane, which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph?

choices.text: The plane reached an estimated maximum height of 10,700 meters 4.9 seconds after it started the parabolic maneuver.
The plane reached an estimated maximum height of 10,700 meters 9.1 seconds after it started the parabolic maneuver.
The plane reached an estimated maximum height of 4.9 meters 10,700 seconds after it started the parabolic maneuver.
The plane reached an estimated maximum height of 9.1 meters 10,700 seconds after it started the parabolic maneuver.

questions.testName: October 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
11 65 7 70 5 75 4 80 3 85 6 90 5 95 10 Total 51
Ari will add a 135-year-old stamp to her original collection, creating a new collection of 52 stamps. Which statement correctly compares the mean ages and median ages of Ari's original and new collections?
questions.questionText:
11 65 7 70 5 75 4 80 3 85 6 90 5 95 10 Total 51
Ari will add a 135-year-old stamp to her original collection, creating a new collection of 52 stamps. Which statement correctly compares the mean ages and median ages of Ari's original and new collections?

choices.text: The mean age for the new collection is greater than the mean age for the original collection, and the median age for the new collection is greater than the median age for the original collection.
The mean age for the new collection is greater than the mean age for the original collection, but the median age is the same for the original and new collections.
The mean age for the new collection is less than the mean age for the original collection, and the median age for the new collection is less than the median age for the original collection.
The median age for the new collection is greater than the median age for the original collection, but the mean age is the same for the original and new collections.

category.subject: Math
category.skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread

questions.testName: October 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A certain open star cluster contains M-type stars and K-type stars. The estimated total mass of M-type and K-type stars in this open star cluster is 129,492 quettagrams. The graph shown models the possible combinations of the number of M-type stars, $x$, and K-type stars, $y$, that could be in this open star cluster if all the M-type stars have the same estimated mass and all the K-type stars have the same estimated mass. Based on the graph, which of the following is closest to the estimated mass, in quettagrams, of each M-type star in this cluster?
questions.questionText: A certain open star cluster contains M-type stars and K-type stars. The estimated total mass of M-type and K-type stars in this open star cluster is 129,492 quettagrams. questions.questionText: A district school board in a certain state is proposing a change to the time school starts for all high schools in the district. A sample of 353 high school students was selected at random from all high school students in the district. The selected students were asked whether they approved of the proposed change, and 300 students responded that they did not approve. Which of the following is the largest population to which the results of the survey can be generalized?

choices.text: All high school students in the district
The 300 students who responded that they did not approve of the proposed change
All high school students in the state

questions.testName: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The Mars Perseverance rover is a robot designed to collect samples of rock and soil. During an experiment, the rover moves at a constant speed on a straight path toward a designated target. The equation $ y = 390 - 142x $ gives the rover's remaining distance from its target $ y $, in meters, $ x $ hours after the start of the experiment. What is the best interpretation of 390 in this context?
questions.questionText: The Mars Perseverance rover is a robot designed to collect samples of rock and soil. During an experiment, the rover moves at a constant speed on a straight path toward a designated target. The equation $ y = 390 - 142x $ gives the rover's remaining distance from its target $ y $, in meters, $ x $ hours after the start of the experiment. What is the best interpretation of 390 in this context?

choices.text: The number of hours it takes the rover to reach its target
The rover's speed, in meters per hour, during the experiment
The rover's distance, in meters, from its target at the start of the experiment
The total distance, in meters, the rover travels in 1 hour

questions.testName: August 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A company that produces only one type of item wants to estimate the percent of the items produced in a typical week that are defective. A random sample of 400 of the items produced in a certain week were tested. Based on the sample, it is estimated that 8.5% of all items produced by the company in this week are defective, with an associated margin of error of 2.73%. Based on the estimate and associated margin of error, which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion about all items produced by the company during this week?
questions.questionText: A company that produces only one type of item wants to estimate the percent of the items produced in a typical week that are defective. A random sample of 400 of the items produced in a certain week were tested. Based on the sample, it is estimated that 8.5% of all items produced by the company in this week are defective, with an associated margin of error of 2.73%. Based on the estimate and associated margin of error, which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion about all items produced by the company during this week?

choices.text: It is plausible that between 5.77% and 11.23% of the items are defective.
It is plausible that more than 11.23% of the items are defective.

questions.questionText: The equation $y = -4.9(x - 9.1)^2 + 10,700$ gives the estimated height above ground, $y$, in meters, of a plane, where $x$ is the number of seconds since it started a parabolic maneuver. The plane reached an estimated maximum height of 10,700 meters 9.1 seconds after it started the parabolic maneuver.

The plane reached an estimated maximum height of 9.1 meters 10,700 seconds after it started the parabolic maneuver.

questions.prompt:
11 65 7 70 5 75 4 80 3 85 6 90 5 95 10 Total 51

questions.questionText:
11 65 7 70 5 75 4 80 3 85 6 90 5 95 10 Total 51
Ari will add a 135-year-old stamp to her original collection, creating a new collection of 52 stamps. Which statement correctly compares the mean ages and median ages of Ari's original and new collections?

choices.text: The mean age for the new collection is greater than the mean age for the original collection, and the median age for the new collection is greater than the median age for the original collection.
The mean age for the new collection is greater than the mean age for the original collection, but the median age is the same for the original and new collections.
The mean age for the new collection is less than the mean age for the original collection, and the median age for the new collection is less than the median age for the original collection.
The median age for the new collection is greater than the median age for the original collection, but the mean age is the same for the original and new collections.
